hints.text: US overseas territory — **counts as US** in country streaks
Car fully blurred, but a **front-right antenna** shows — noticeably **closer to the camera** than Guam's, and no roof rack

shared_with.note: Guam shows a roof-rack bit + more distant antenna.

hints.text: **Wooden poles**, frequently w/ a top crossbar
Tropical, dense low vegetation; the **Flame Tree** (Royal Poinciana — wide crown w/ bright red flowers) is the official tree & very common (NOT common in Guam)
hints.uniq_note: Red-flowered flame trees in a Pacific-US setting → NMI over Guam.

shared_with.note: Guam has a similarly dense tropical vegetation overall, but lacks the red-flowered flame trees.
shared_with.diff: Flame trees present → NMI; absent in an otherwise identical-looking round → lean Guam.

hints.text: Huge numbers of **road reflectors** + **East Asian languages** (JA/ZH/KO/RU) on signs — both also true of Guam
Only **Saipan** has car coverage (northern islands: none; Tinian & Sinapalu: tripods only); dense commercial areas → southern half of the west coast
**Suicide Cliff**: steep cliff w/ lots of exposed rock near Saipan's northern tip
